title: React.memo
body:
## Definition

**React.memo** wraps a component to skip [re-render](concept:react-re-render) when props are shallowly equal to the previous render.

## Why it exists

Cut unnecessary work for pure presentational components with stable props.

## How it works

```js
const Row = memo(function Row({ item }) { ... });
```

Custom `arePropsEqual` optional. Compares each prop with `Object.is`.

## Common confusion

New object/function props every parent render break memo — pair with [useMemo](concept:use-memo)/[useCallback](concept:use-callback). Memo adds comparison overhead.

## In practice

List rows, heavy charts, icon components — profile before sprinkling memo everywhere.

## Definition

**[React.memo](concept:react-memo)** wraps a component to skip [re-render](concept:react-re-render) when props are shallowly equal to the previous render.

## Why it exists

Cut unnecessary work for pure presentational components with stable props.

## How it works

```js
const Row = memo(function Row({ item }) { ... });
```

Custom `arePropsEqual` optional. Compares each prop with `Object.is`.

## Common confusion

[New](concept:new-operator) object/function props [every](concept:array-some-every) parent render break [memo](concept:react-memo) — pair with [useMemo](concept:use-memo)/[useCallback](concept:use-callback). [Memo](concept:react-memo) adds comparison overhead.

## In practice

List rows, heavy charts, icon components — profile before sprinkling [memo](concept:react-memo) everywhere.
